preamble: The Slavic Second Palatalization involved the shift of [ɡ] to [dz] before certain front vowels.

examples.note: Proto-Slavic *g became /dz/ (later /z/ in many Slavic languages) before front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
